description: Letter of appeal for charity. Addressed to someone titled al-Amīniyya. Probably from a cantor, although theoretically al-Ḥazzān could be part of the addressee's name. In Judaeo-Arabic. Recto is entirely greetings and good wishes for the holiday. Verso presents the writer's case and his pressing need for money for the holidays. He sends regards to Abū l-Ṭāhir Amīn al-Dawla.
description_he: מכתב בקשת צדקה; לאמינייה; כנראה מחזן; ביהודית-ערבית; פנים: ברכות לחג; גב: מצבו הקשה וצורך דחוף בכסף לחג; שולח ברכות לאבו אל-טאהר
